metadata.purpose: Assess prerequisite knowledge before learning about 3D shapes, faces, edges, vertices, angles, and identifying shapes around us

prerequisiteConcepts.conceptName: Counting sides and corners of shapes
Understanding the difference between flat and solid shapes

questions.question: Which shape has 3 sides and 3 corners?
questions.explanation: A triangle has exactly 3 sides and 3 corners. The word 'tri' means three!

commonMistakes.conceptGap: A square has 4 sides and 4 corners, not 3
commonMistakes.remediation: Count sides of common shapes using pictures

questions.question: Which shape is perfectly round with no sides and no corners?
questions.explanation: A circle is perfectly round. It has no straight sides and no corners at all!

questions.question: A square and a rectangle both have 4 sides. What is special about a square?
options: All 4 sides are the same length
questions.correctAnswerText: All 4 sides are the same length
questions.explanation: A square is special because all 4 of its sides are the same length. A rectangle can have longer sides and shorter sides.

commonMistakes.conceptGap: Shape type depends on side lengths and angles, not overall size
commonMistakes.remediation: Compare different sized squares and rectangles

questions.question: How many sides does a rectangle have?
questions.explanation: A rectangle has 4 sides: two longer sides and two shorter sides.

questions.question: A shape has 5 sides and 5 corners. What is this shape called?
questions.explanation: A pentagon has 5 sides and 5 corners. 'Penta' means five!

commonMistakes.remediation: Practice counting sides on different polygon pictures

questions.question: Rina drew a shape with 4 equal sides and 4 corners. Which shape did she draw?
questions.explanation: A square has 4 equal sides (all the same length) and 4 corners. A rectangle also has 4 sides and 4 corners, but its sides are not all equal.

commonMistakes.conceptGap: The key word is 'equal' sides - a rectangle has 4 sides but not all equal
commonMistakes.remediation: Compare square and rectangle side by side, measuring sides

questions.question: Which of these is a flat shape that you can draw on paper?
questions.explanation: A triangle is a flat (2D) shape that you can draw on paper. A ball, box, and tin can are solid (3D) shapes that take up space.

questions.question: True or False: A ball is a flat shape because it is round like a circle.
questions.explanation: False! A ball is a solid (3D) shape called a sphere. You can hold it in your hands. A circle is the flat (2D) version - you can draw it on paper but cannot hold it.

questions.question: What shape is the face of a clock?
questions.explanation: Most clocks have a round face, which is a circle shape!

questions.question: Look at a door. What shape is it?
questions.explanation: A door is shaped like a rectangle - it has 4 sides with 2 longer sides and 2 shorter sides.

commonMistakes.conceptGap: A door is taller than it is wide, making it a rectangle, not a square
commonMistakes.remediation: Measure real objects to see if all sides are equal (square) or not (rectangle)

excellent.message: Excellent! You know your shapes very well. You are ready to explore 3D shapes and their properties!

good.message: Good job! You have a solid understanding of shapes. Let's learn about 3D shapes, faces, edges, and angles!

partial.message: You know some shapes but need to review a few things first. Watch the review videos and you will be all set!

needs_review.message: Let's first review basic shapes, sides, and corners. We have some fun videos to help you get ready!

feedbackMessages.on_completion: Great job completing the readiness check!
feedbackMessages.low_score_encouragement: Don't worry! We'll show you some fun videos about shapes to help you get ready.
feedbackMessages.high_score_praise: Awesome! You're all set to learn about 3D shapes and angles!
feedbackMessages.retake_option: After watching the recommended videos, you can try again to check your progress.
